For each repo in shared/repos.json: list PRs merged this week (`gh pr list --state merged --search 'merged:>={{7_DAYS_AGO}}'`), closed issues, and notable commits. Write findings to shared/inbox/main/ with subject 'Weekly Dev Summary'.
Compile this week's notable research findings from memory files and FreshRSS. Highlight top 3-5 items worth remembering. Write to shared/inbox/main/ with subject 'Weekly Research Summary'.

Read weekly summaries from dev and research. Compile into a 'Week in Review' report covering: (1) code shipped, (2) issues resolved, (3) notable research/news, (4) upcoming priorities. Send to Anthony via Telegram.
steps.output:
{
  "runId": "305c4d7c-6ab7-46ab-b1fb-0cedae411ee7",
  "status": "ok",
  "summary": "completed",
  "result": {
    "payloads": [
      {
        "text": "**Week in Review**\n\n1. **Code shipped**\n- Merged PR: **Add functional power workflows with execution tracking**\n- Shipped workflow reliability fixes:\n- strip `OPENCLAW` env vars from child processes\n- switch workflow executor shell handling to `execFile`\n- deliver the last workflow step via Telegram through the gateway\n- Dashboard / agent workflow improvements this week:\n- clickable **Run** button for power workflows\n- wired up power workflows, cron jobs, heartbeat state tracking, and dashboard enhancements\n- multiple agent discovery fixes and fallback improvements\n- dashboard cleanup and visual refresh work, including the RPG guild hall / Mission Control theme\n\n2. **Issues resolved**\n- No GitHub issues were explicitly closed this week.\n- But several practical problems were fixed in code:\n- workflow executor escaping bugs\n- env leakage into child processes\n- Telegram delivery for final workflow step\n- agent discovery failures when the repo/config/gateway fallback path was incomplete\n- dashboard dead pages / debug-route cleanup\n\n3. **Notable research / news**\n- **AI infrastructure is now a core bottleneck**: grid capacity, transformers, batteries, demand response, and virtual power plants are becoming central to AI deployment.\n- **Consumer AI is moving toward supervised actions**, not just chat: Google’s Pixel/Gemini updates point toward task execution inside apps.\n- **AI procurement and policy are getting more strategic**: the Pentagon/Anthropic conflict highlighted how frontier AI competition is getting tied to government access, trust, and national-security procurement.\n- **Enterprise/government AI is institutionalizing**: the story is shifting from “model demo” to governance, deployment, permissions, workflow management, and shared organizational context.\n- **Vertical AI remains underrated**: embedded systems like automotive AI may matter as much as chatbot products.\n\n4. **Upcoming priorities**\n- Stabilize and polish the new functional power-workflow system now that it’s merged.\n- Keep tightening workflow execution reliability and message delivery.\n- Clean up outstanding repo changes and dashboard-related in-progress work.\n- Watch the infrastructure/power side of AI as a leading signal for what can actually scale.\n- Track the shift from assistant UX to constrained agentic execution, especially in mobile and enterprise settings.\n\n**Bottom line:**\nThis week was about turning OpenClaw workflows into something more operational and shippable, while the broader research signal says AI’s next phase will be determined less by raw model novelty and more by infrastructure, deployment systems, and institutional adoption.",
        "mediaUrl": null
      }
    ],

steps.action: Investigate the CI failure. Identify the failing job, error message, and likely cause. Write findings to shared/inbox/main/ with subject 'CI Failure Report' and priority 'high'.
